proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher feature_015 (continuous) is associated with longer pfs_months across the cohort.
Higher feature_014 (ordinal 0/1/2, ECOG-like) is associated with shorter pfs_months across the cohort.
Patients with feature_023=1 have shorter pfs_months than patients with feature_023=0.
Patients with feature_011=1 have shorter pfs_months than patients with feature_011=0.
Patients with feature_006='squamous' have shorter pfs_months than patients with feature_006='adenocarcinoma'.
Patients with feature_016=1 have longer pfs_months than patients with feature_016=0.
Patients with feature_028=1 have shorter pfs_months than patients with feature_028=0.
Higher feature_017 (continuous) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher feature_025 (continuous) is associated with longer pfs_months.
Smoking status feature_001='current' is associated with shorter pfs_months than 'former' or 'never'.
Patients with feature_018=1 have longer pfs_months than patients with feature_018=0 (overall main effect).
Patients with feature_012=1 differ from feature_012=0 in pfs_months (overall main effect).

analyses.result_summary: Pearson r = +0.7571, p < 1e-300; very strong positive correlation. feature_015 is the dominant continuous prognostic in the cohort.
Mean PFS: feature_014=0 4.33; =1 3.19; =2 2.15. ANOVA p ≈ 0; monotone decrease with feature_014.
feature_023=1 vs 0: mean PFS 2.91 vs 4.35 (Δ=-1.44 months), p ≈ 0.
feature_011=1 vs 0: mean PFS 2.74 vs 3.66 (Δ=-0.92 months), p ≈ 0.
adenocarcinoma 3.67 vs squamous 2.81 months (Δ=-0.86), p ≈ 0.
Pearson r = -0.127, p=7.5e-179; modest negative association.
Pearson r = +0.099, p=2.8e-109; modest positive association.
current 2.99, former 3.55, never 3.66. Current smoker has lowest PFS; ANOVA p=3.7e-187.
feature_012=1 vs 0: mean PFS 3.41 vs 3.45 (Δ=-0.035), p=0.081 — null overall.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_012, feature_018, feature_020, and feature_027 are independently allocated treatment-like binary variables (mutual correlation ≈ 0).
feature_018 has a positive overall main effect on pfs_months while feature_012, feature_020, feature_027 have null main effects.

analyses.result_summary: All pairwise correlations among the 4 candidates ≤ |0.008|, consistent with independent allocation. Allocations: f012 50.0%, f018 35.1%, f020 30.0%, f027 30.0%.
Main effects: feature_018 +0.32 (p=2e-53), feature_012 -0.03 (p=0.08), feature_020 -0.02 (p=0.25), feature_027 +0.004 (p=0.87). Only feature_018 has a clear main effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A multivariable OLS using all features explains a large share of pfs_months variance, with feature_015, feature_014, feature_023, feature_011, feature_006(squamous), and feature_016 as the leading prognostic factors.
After adjustment for prognostic features, the main effect of feature_018 remains positive while feature_012, feature_020, feature_027 main effects remain near zero.

analyses.result_summary: R² = 0.894. Top |t|: feature_015 t=+512 (β=+0.168/unit), feature_014 t=-230 (β=-1.10), feature_023 t=-208 (β=-1.42), feature_011 t=-122 (β=-0.91), squamous histology t=-95 (β=-0.72), feature_016 t=+90 (β=+0.89), feature_017 t=-87 (β=-0.073), feature_025 t=+71 (β=+0.46), current smoker t=-48.
Adjusted main effects: feature_018 β=+0.323 (p≈0); feature_012 β=-0.006 (p=0.39); feature_020 β≈0 (NS); feature_027 β≈0 (NS). Only feature_018 retains a significant adjusted main effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_018 has a strong positive interaction with feature_016 (the binary biomarker): the feature_018 effect on pfs_months is much larger when feature_016=1.
feature_018 has a strong negative interaction with feature_031: the feature_018 effect is reduced (or abolished) when feature_031=1.
feature_012, feature_020, and feature_027 show only weak interactions with any feature.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction β = +2.558 months, p ≈ 0. Within feature_016=1 the feature_018 effect is +2.55 months; within feature_016=0 it is essentially zero.
Interaction β = -0.562 months, p=5.3e-41. The feature_018 effect is +0.57 months in feature_031=0 vs ~0 in feature_031=1.

Top interaction p-values: feature_012:feature_017 p=3.2e-4 (β=+0.071); feature_020:feature_029 p=0.014 (β=+0.054); feature_027:feature_028 p=0.017 (β=+0.24). Effect magnitudes are small relative to feature_018:feature_016 (β=+2.56).

proposed_hypotheses.text: In feature_016=1 patients, mean pfs_months is substantially higher when feature_018=1 than feature_018=0; in feature_016=0 patients there is no difference.
Within feature_016=1, the feature_018 effect on pfs_months is further modified by feature_031: patients with feature_031=0 receive the full benefit; patients with feature_031=1 receive no benefit.
Within feature_016=1, the feature_018 effect is also abolished when feature_028=1 or feature_005=1.

analyses.result_summary: Within f016=1: f018:f028 interaction β=-3.02, p=2.9e-18; f018:f005 interaction β=-2.55, p=2.3e-10. Both gates abolish the feature_018 benefit when set to 1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In the joint subgroup feature_016=1 & feature_031=0 & feature_028=0 & feature_005=0, feature_018=1 produces ≈ +5 months PFS gain compared with feature_018=0.
Outside this subgroup, the feature_018 effect on pfs_months is essentially zero.
feature_031 is the dominant suppressor; feature_028 and feature_005 are minor refinements (each affects only ~5% of feature_016=1 patients).

analyses.result_summary: In SG_018 (n=3266): mean PFS T1=8.23 vs T0=3.25, Δ=+4.99 months, p≈0.

Subgroup f016=1 & gates(other)=0: dropping f031 gate drops effect to +2.74 (n=5926). Dropping f028 gate barely changes effect (+4.80, n=3401). Dropping f005 gate barely changes effect (+4.83, n=3370). f031 carries the main suppressor signal.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effect of feature_012 on pfs_months is small in magnitude even within candidate biomarker subgroups; no subgroup yields a clinically large effect.
feature_012 has a small negative effect concentrated in feature_023=0 (non-metastatic) patients.

analyses.code: stratified ttest of feature_012 by tertiles/binaries
analyses.result_summary: Best feature_012 effects from a 2-binary-gate scan: feature_023=0 & feature_016=1 → Δ=-0.46 (p=1.5e-4), feature_031=0 & feature_016=1 → Δ=-0.25 (p=0.02). Magnitudes are small and disappear after covariate adjustment.
Adjusted feature_012 in f023=0: β=-0.030 (p=0.007), interaction f012:f023 β=+0.037 (p=0.007). Statistically detectable but tiny in magnitude (≈0.03 months).

proposed_hypotheses.text: A regression tree fit on the IPTW-transformed outcome confirms feature_018 has dramatic heterogeneity centered on feature_016 then feature_031, while feature_012, feature_020, feature_027 show only minor leaf-level effects.
Within the active subgroup, the feature_018 effect grows with feature_015 (a continuous prognostic).

analyses.result_summary: feature_018 tree: top split on feature_016, then feature_031, then feature_015 — leaf effects up to +9.0 within f016=1 & f031=0. feature_012/_020/_027 trees have largest leaf effects ±2 months in tiny subgroups (probable overfit); main leaves close to 0.
In f016=1 & f031=0, interaction feature_018:feature_015 β=+0.30 per SD, p=1.9e-9. Effect grows from +4.64 in feature_015 Q1 to +5.10 in Q4 (within active subgroup).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_028 within the active subgroup is the strongest single binary suppressor of feature_018 (interaction β ≈ -5.1 within f016=1 & f031=0).
feature_005 within the active subgroup is the second strongest binary suppressor of feature_018 (interaction β ≈ -4.9 within f016=1 & f031=0).
feature_023 (stage) and feature_006 (histology) do not modify the feature_018 effect within the active subgroup.

analyses.result_summary: feature_018:feature_028 interaction β = -5.12, p=1.4e-35. Within f016=1 & f031=0: f028=1 patients lose all benefit.
feature_018:feature_005 interaction β = -4.86, p=8.2e-26. Within f016=1 & f031=0: f005=1 patients lose all benefit.
In SG_018=1: adenocarcinoma Δ=+5.09, squamous Δ=+4.85; f023=0 Δ=+5.19, f023=1 Δ=+4.91. Differences are negligible (<0.3 mo).

proposed_hypotheses.text: After covariate adjustment for all major prognostic factors, the feature_018 × SG_018 interaction effect on pfs_months is approximately +4.9 months (95% CI ~[4.90, 4.95]).
The subgroup definition replicates on a random 50/50 split: in-subgroup effect ~+5 mo on both halves; out-subgroup effect ~0 on both.
Adding feature_012, feature_020, or feature_027 to feature_018 within the active subgroup does not change the treatment benefit appreciably.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted interaction β = +4.926 months, t=397, p<<1e-300. Adjusted main feature_018 outside SG_018 = +0.0005 (NS). SG_018 main effect ≈ 0 (NS).

feature_012:feature_018 within SG_018 β=-0.32 (p=0.04, mild). feature_020:feature_018 β=+0.06 (NS). feature_027:feature_018 β=+0.20 (NS). No combination amplifies the feature_018 benefit; modest negative interaction with feature_012.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_022, feature_021, feature_028, feature_011, feature_023 are not independently allocated treatments (they are prognostic / clinical features) — no large interaction signal.
No alternative treatment indicator with a +5-month subgroup-level effect exists in this dataset; feature_018 is the unique high-effect treatment.

analyses.result_summary: For T in {f022, f021, f028, f011, f023}, only routine prognostic-by-prognostic interactions surface (e.g., f023:f015 β=-0.13 per SD, p=2e-25 — a stage×age effect, not treatment heterogeneity).
No other binary feature × biomarker interaction reproduces the magnitude of feature_018 × feature_016 (β=+2.56) or the +5-month subgroup effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_018 prevalence is constant (≈35%) across (feature_016, feature_031) cells, ruling out confounding-by-indication for the subgroup effect.
After covariate adjustment, the feature_018 effect within SG_018=1 is +4.92 months (SE 0.010), uniform across histology, stage, and smoking status.
feature_012 has no detectable effect within feature_016=1, feature_016=1 & f023=0, or feature_016=1 & f031=0 after full adjustment.

analyses.result_summary: feature_018 prevalence: f016=0,f031=0: 35.4%; f016=0,f031=1: 34.6%; f016=1,f031=0: 35.3%; f016=1,f031=1: 35.3%. Independent allocation confirmed.

Adjusted: β_feature_018 = +4.924 (SE 0.010, t=514). By histology: adeno +5.09, squamous +4.85. By stage: f023=0 +5.19, f023=1 +4.91. By smoking: never +4.32, former +5.21, current +4.76. Highly consistent.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Best-supported treatment-effect subgroup hypothesis (final): feature_018=1 (vs 0) increases pfs_months by approximately +5 months in patients defined by feature_016=1 AND feature_031=0 AND feature_028=0 AND feature_005=0; in any patient violating these criteria, feature_018 has no detectable effect on pfs_months.

Among the four candidate treatments examined (feature_012, feature_018, feature_020, feature_027), only feature_018 has clinically meaningful treatment heterogeneity. feature_012, feature_020, feature_027 lack any subgroup with a clinically meaningful (>0.5 mo) treatment effect after covariate adjustment.

analyses.result_summary: Final subgroup feature_016=1 & feature_031=0 & feature_028=0 & feature_005=0: n=3266, adjusted feature_018 effect = +4.924 mo (95% CI 4.91–4.94, p≈0). Outside subgroup: adjusted effect = +0.0005 (NS). Held-out validation effect = +4.95 mo. Three-way and four-way subgroup definitions confirm feature_031 as dominant suppressor and feature_028, feature_005 as additional suppressors with the same direction.

After full adjustment and subgroup search: feature_018 → +4.92 mo in 6.5% subgroup. feature_012 → no adjusted effect anywhere (|β|<0.07). feature_020 → no adjusted effect anywhere. feature_027 → small effects only in tiny <5% subsets, not robust.
